purpose: Executable inventory of exported or subsystem-visible contracts whose current implementation is link-compatible but not full POSIX, GNU, or glibc semantics. This extends support_semantic_overlay.v1.json from representative drift into a countable backlog input.

semantic_contract_classes.compat_noop: Call is accepted and returns success or no observable action for compatibility, while upstream behavior is not implemented.
semantic_contract_classes.deterministic_fallback: Call returns a deterministic fallback/error/fail-stop result instead of full upstream behavior.
semantic_contract_classes.limited_bootstrap: Implementation supports a useful bootstrap subset, but broader upstream subsystem semantics remain deferred.
semantic_contract_classes.unsupported_platform_contract: Historical, non-Linux, removed, or deliberately unavailable surface returns ENOSYS or an equivalent unavailable status.
semantic_contract_classes.abort_only: Surface intentionally fails closed through abort or process termination instead of performing full upstream behavior.
semantic_contract_classes.proof_gap: Surface is link-compatible and taxonomy-implemented, but a known semantic gap lacks fixture/proof evidence for release-level parity.

entries.current_behavior: RelocationSupport includes Stub and Unsupported variants even though exported-symbol taxonomy reports Stub=0.
entries.user_risk: A release claim based only on exported symbols can overstate loader readiness for binaries that need relocation classes outside the phase-1 subset.
entries.required_followup: Gate replacement claims on per-relocation class coverage and fixture execution, not only exported-symbol status counts.

entries.current_behavior: Registration and cleanup helpers are no-op compatibility hooks; __pthread_unwind_next aborts instead of performing glibc cancellation unwinding.
entries.user_risk: Thread-cancellation consumers can link but cleanup/unwind behavior is not semantically equivalent.
entries.required_followup: Add cancellation cleanup and unwind fixtures before any L1 replacement promotion.

entries.current_behavior: DES block and key-schedule helpers are no-op compatibility stubs.
entries.user_risk: Legacy crypt callers that expect DES mutation can silently receive no transformation.
entries.required_followup: Keep the no-op contract explicit in fixtures or implement DES semantics before counting this family as crypt parity.

entries.surface: NSS files module private plugin entries
entries.current_behavior: Files plugin entries return SUCCESS or NOTFOUND/ENOENT style deterministic results without opening full NSS backing stores.
entries.user_risk: NSS module consumers can link while module iteration, parsing, and backend fallthrough are only approximated.
entries.required_followup: Add exhaustive NSS private-entry inventory and fixtures that distinguish bootstrap behavior from files/compat/hesiod/systemd backend semantics.

entries.line_marker: instead of glibc's NSS module system
entries.current_behavior: NSS dispatch internals are collapsed into native file parsing and UNAVAIL/null fallbacks rather than glibc's module chain.
entries.user_risk: Programs relying on nsswitch module ordering, dynamic services, or fallback-name dispatch can observe divergence.
entries.required_followup: Gate NSS maturity on nsswitch ordering, module loading, backend fallthrough, concurrency, and cache-coherence fixtures.

entries.current_behavior: Narrow libio-style vtable helpers route through native _IO file overflow and underflow paths; wide vtable helpers return deterministic WEOF fallbacks.
entries.user_risk: Wide _IO helper paths still fail instead of driving full glibc stream internals.
entries.required_followup: Separate link compatibility from libio semantic parity and add fixtures for the remaining wide _IO helper paths.

entries.surface: removed or unsupported historical syscall wrappers
entries.current_behavior: Removed or unsupported Linux interfaces deliberately return ENOSYS-style contracts.
entries.user_risk: Legacy binaries get a deterministic unavailable result, not historical kernel behavior.
entries.required_followup: Keep these rows explicit as unsupported platform contracts and exclude them from behavior-complete replacement claims.

entries.current_behavior: mcheck and mtrace setup/teardown hooks are accepted as safe no-ops while mprobe delegates to allocator metadata.
entries.user_risk: Programs expecting glibc mcheck/mtrace reporting do not receive those diagnostics.
entries.required_followup: Track these as membrane-substituted compatibility contracts with explicit tests and exclude them from glibc debug-hook parity counts.

entries.surface: GNU argp help and reporting helpers
entries.current_behavior: argp_help prints bounded literal usage/doc sections to supplied streams, argp_state_help bridges state.root_argp/name into the same renderer without process exit, argp_usage renders standard usage through state.err_stream without process exit, and argp_error/failure render bounded printf-style diagnostics to state.err_stream/state.out_stream when a non-null state stream is available while null-state fixture rows remain deterministic no-ops.
entries.user_risk: Command-line programs can still lose option-table help, child-parser/filter behavior, full parser callback diagnostics, and argp-triggered process exits while appearing link-compatible.
entries.required_followup: Add full argp parser/help fixtures for options, child parsers, filters, complete state stream selection, and process-exit behavior before claiming complete GNU argp parity.

entries.current_behavior: Regular-mutex priority-ceiling get/set return EINVAL without writing out-parameters; mutexattr priority-ceiling get/set are native managed-attribute round trips covered outside this blocker.
entries.user_risk: Realtime pthread users can link, but PRIO_PROTECT mutex priority-ceiling behavior remains unavailable for regular mutex objects.
entries.required_followup: Add POSIX PRIO_PROTECT mutex fixtures if priority-protocol mutex semantics are implemented; keep mutexattr priority-ceiling round trips out of unsupported semantic blockers.

entries.current_behavior: Instrumentation hooks are accepted as no-ops or success-no-ops because profiling data is unused.
entries.user_risk: Profiling-enabled binaries can start, but gprof-style data collection is absent.
entries.required_followup: Add a replacement-level decision: either document profiling as unsupported or implement compatible profiling output.

entries.current_behavior: Private gconv step-chain APIs expose safe-default descriptors, null databases, and public-iconv-adjacent behavior rather than glibc module internals.
entries.user_risk: Programs reaching GLIBC_PRIVATE gconv internals can link but may not observe glibc cache/module/transliteration semantics.
entries.required_followup: Gate iconv maturity on codec-table coverage, transliteration fixtures, gconv cache behavior, and replacement-level policy.

entries.surface: NIS and Yellow Pages fail-safe libnsl entries
entries.line_marker: NIS / Yellow Pages is dead infrastructure
entries.current_behavior: NIS runtime calls collapse to no-domain or unreachable deterministic errors instead of contacting NIS services.
entries.user_risk: Legacy network identity consumers can link but will not resolve NIS records.
entries.required_followup: Keep this as an explicit unsupported/failsafe replacement-level decision, with fixtures for errno/status stability.

entries.surface: NIS XDR encoder and decoder helpers
entries.current_behavior: NIS XDR helpers fail closed with XDR_FALSE instead of serializing real NIS protocol structures.
entries.user_risk: RPC/NIS protocol consumers can link but cannot perform real encode/decode operations.
entries.required_followup: Either implement XDR structures or mark this family as link-only for replacement-level claims.

entries.current_behavior: Degenerate synchronous request shapes are supported, while true async resolver work is treated as unsupported with ENOSYS/EAI_SYSTEM paths.
entries.user_risk: Programs expecting background getaddrinfo_a requests do not receive asynchronous behavior.
entries.required_followup: Add strict/hardened async resolver fixtures for pending, canceled, done, invalid, and timeout paths before parity promotion.

entries.current_behavior: Capture entrypoints write deterministic metadata, while restore entrypoints validate metadata and terminate explicitly until true stack transfer is implemented in the unsafe backend stage.
entries.user_risk: Programs can link setjmp-family symbols, but full non-local stack transfer semantics are not yet replacement-complete.
entries.required_followup: Gate setjmp parity promotion on backend stack-transfer fixtures, signal-mask restoration cases, and process-level longjmp behavior evidence.

entries.line_marker: C99 `nexttoward`: next representable f64 toward a long-double direction.
entries.current_behavior: The ABI accepts nexttoward-family direction values as f64 and delegates through nextafter-style f64 paths, so long-double-only direction differences can be lost before core math dispatch.
entries.user_risk: Math callers can observe no step when the true long-double direction differs from x only outside f64 precision.
entries.required_followup: Implement 80-bit long-double ABI plumbing and add directed nexttoward fixtures for direction values that round to x in f64 before semantic parity promotion.

entries.surface: C++ ABI fail-stop hooks exported for compatibility
entries.current_behavior: C++ ABI violation hooks write diagnostics or directly abort, matching fail-stop linkage needs but not implementing a full C++ runtime.
entries.user_risk: C++ binaries can resolve these hooks, but exception/runtime semantics still depend on the surrounding C++ runtime.
entries.required_followup: Keep abort-only hooks separate from libc semantic parity and cover them with process-exit fixtures if replacement claims mention C++ runtime compatibility.

summary.blocked_claim: Stub=0 in support_matrix.json is compatible with this inventory only as a symbol-presence taxonomy. It is not evidence of full semantic replacement readiness.
